message:
feat(package-manager): recognize JS/TS files as valid extension entrypoints

Updates the package manager's extension discovery and validation logic to
accept JavaScript and TypeScript files as first-class extension entrypoints,
complementing the re-enabled QuickJS runtime.

is_supported_extension_file():
- Expand from only accepting .wasm to also accepting .ts, .tsx, .js, .mjs,
  .cjs, .mts, .cts extensions, matching the set recognized by
  resolve_extension_load_spec() in extensions.rs

resolve_extension_entries():
- Replace the previous behavior of warning and ignoring index.ts/index.js
  with a loop that checks all JS/TS index variants (index.ts, .tsx, .js,
  .mjs, .cjs, .mts, .cts) and returns the first match as a valid entrypoint
- This restores the ability to discover JS extensions in extension directories
  without requiring an index.native.json descriptor

collect_auto_extension_entries():
- Replace the narrow .native.json filename check with a call to
  is_supported_extension_file(), so standalone JS/TS files in the extensions
  directory are auto-discovered alongside native descriptors
- Add sort() + dedup() to prevent duplicate entries when both an index file
  and a directory-level scan find the same path

Warning messages:
- Update four warning strings to mention "JS/TS entrypoints" as a supported
  format alongside extension.json, *.native.json, and *.wasm

Tests:
- collect_auto_extension_entries_finds_root_js_entry_file: verifies a bare
  .ts file in the extensions directory is discovered
- collect_auto_extension_entries_deduplicates_index_entry: verifies an
  index.ts found by both the index-file check and the directory walk appears
  only once in the output
- resolve_extension_entries_finds_index_ts: updated from _ignores_ to
  _finds_ (behavior reversal)
- resolve_extension_entries_finds_index_js: same update

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
